expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L34-36 shows '## Ownership map' followed by table header '| Concern | Owner | Notes |'.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L93-101 '## Conflict resolution' covers visual-vs-copy, policy-vs-prose, implementation-vs-policy, platform-choice, and CAP/ASA arbitration edges. Presented as a bulleted list rather than a table, but addresses boundary edges explicitly.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a named Sizing Rationale section defending the chosen number of agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L30 has '## Sizing rationale' defending four agents vs user's three-agent proposal by naming the orphaned UK-alcohol-licensed-commerce compliance concern.

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, tools, model, and memory fields (path substitution OK for sandbox-blocked .claude/)
expectations.evidence: agents/brand-lead.md, content-lead.md, web-engineer.md, taproom-ops.md each have frontmatter L1-7 with name, description, tools, model, memory (e.g. brand-lead.md L2-6).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: Line 6 of each of the four agent files reads 'memory: project' (verified in brand-lead.md, content-lead.md, web-engineer.md, taproom-ops.md).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger (specifies when to invoke, mentions exclusions) — not a generic bio
expectations.evidence: Each description uses 'Use when...' plus 'Do NOT use for...' exclusions (e.g. taproom-ops.md L3 'Use when the user asks about premises licence conditions... Do NOT use to WRITE the prose of T&Cs...').

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: All four files have headings ## Identity, ## Voice, ## Analytical Frameworks, ## Boundaries, ## Failure Modes, ## Standing Instructions (e.g. brand-lead.md L9, L13, L21, L29, L40, L48).

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: Each file's Boundaries section has **Owns:**, **Does not own:**, **Hands off to:** (e.g. brand-lead.md L31, L33, L35; same pattern across content-lead, web-engineer, taproom-ops).

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L76 explicitly states 'No concern owned by two agents.' Each row in the L36-74 ownership-map table lists a single owner; notes like 'Prose is content-lead' on taproom-ops rows clarify split responsibility without shared ownership.

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox (obsolete pattern)
expectations.evidence: ls of outputs/ shows only .claude/, agents/, agent-calibration/, COMPANY.md, activation-summary.md; no agent-company/ directory.

expectations.text: Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under .claude/agent-calibration/ or agent-calibration/ (never elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: Both agent-calibration/ (outputs root) and .claude/agent-calibration/ directories exist and are empty; no calibration transcripts persisted elsewhere — calibration results are reported inline in activation-summary.md L16-66, which is not a transcript. Vacuously satisfied.

expectations.text: The executor challenged the user's initial 3-agent suggestion rather than blindly accepting it
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L32 'The user proposed three agents — design, content, web-dev — which is a natural first split but leaves a material concern orphaned: UK alcohol-licensed commerce compliance.' Four-agent roster justified.

expectations.text: SEO or visibility is explicitly addressed — as its own agent, or assigned in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md L48 'SEO keyword strategy (organic) | content-lead' and L59 'Schema markup, sitemap, robots | web-engineer' — SEO split between content-lead and web-engineer in the ownership map.

expectations.text: An activation summary artefact exists (activation-summary.md or equivalent) reporting what was built, calibration results per agent, and routing sanity check result
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md exists with '## Files written' (L3), per-agent calibration tables L20-63, and '## Routing sanity check' L68-96.

expectations.text: The routing sanity check was run and its result (clean or flagged prompts) is reported in the activation summary
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md L72-85 reports 12 prompts run twice; prompt 11 initially flagged as ambiguous between content-lead and taproom-ops, resolved by description edits (L87-96 '### Flag resolved'); re-run clean.

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content (not placeholder text)
expectations.evidence: Each Voice section has two block-quoted concrete examples (e.g. brand-lead.md L17, L19 'strong ascender'/'objects, not stickers'; taproom-ops.md L17, L19 'Royal Mail won't carry alcohol'/'Challenge 25').

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: All four files name 3 frameworks with concrete criteria, e.g. web-engineer.md L23-27 (Core Web Vitals budget with LCP<2.5s/CLS<0.1/INP<200ms, WCAG 2.2 AA checklist, Buy-vs-build matrix); taproom-ops.md L23-27 (Licensing Act 2003, HMRC duty + SPR, Alcohol e-commerce compliance stack).

expectations.text: At least one framework per agent names a domain-specific source (regulator, named methodology, official standard, published benchmark) — not a generic business tool
expectations.evidence: brand-lead.md L25 names 'Portman Group Code'; content-lead.md L23 names 'CAP Code (non-broadcast) Section 18'; web-engineer.md L23 names 'Core Web Vitals' (Google) and L25 'WCAG 2.2 AA'; taproom-ops.md L23 'Licensing Act 2003 + Bristol licensing policy' and L25 'HMRC Small Producer Relief'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: Each file lists 5-6 role-specific failure modes, e.g. content-lead.md L42-46 covers tasting-note generic descriptors, CAP/ASA memory-quoting, unverified SEO volume, orphan blog posts, labelling-copy memory-quoting.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: brand-lead.md L44 'Citing the current Portman Group Code or ASA/CAP alcohol-advertising rules from memory'; content-lead.md L43 'Quoting the current CAP Code or ASA ruling precedents from memory'; web-engineer.md L45 'Quoting current Shopify/Stripe/payment-processor fee structures... from memory'; taproom-ops.md L42 'Quoting the current UK alcohol duty rate... from memory'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
expectations.evidence: brand-lead.md L51 'cite URLs before signing off'; content-lead.md L51 'cite URLs'; web-engineer.md L52 'cite URLs before committing'; taproom-ops.md L52 'cite URLs every time'.

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
expectations.evidence: Each file's first Standing Instruction bullet says 'Consult persistent memory at session start...' and 'Save [a log entry] after...' (brand-lead.md L50, content-lead.md L50, web-engineer.md L51, taproom-ops.md L51).

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
expectations.evidence: brand-lead.md L52 'Reference `COMPANY.md` at project root for the brand/content/web boundary edges'; content-lead.md L52 same; web-engineer.md L53 'Reference `COMPANY.md` for boundary edges'; taproom-ops.md L53 'Reference `COMPANY.md` for boundary edges'.

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: Cross-check: COMPANY.md L38-50 (brand-lead rows: logo, colour, typography, photography, labels, merch, brand guidelines) matches brand-lead.md L31 Owns. L45-52 content-lead rows (site copy, products, blog, SEO, metadata, newsletter, T&C prose, CAP/ASA written) match content-lead.md L31. L53-63 web-engineer rows match web-engineer.md L31. L64-74 taproom-ops rows match taproom-ops.md L31. No concern appears in two Owns sections.

expectations.text: Tools field is scoped per agent (writer agents lack Bash; research agents lack Edit unless justified)
expectations.evidence: brand-lead.md L4 'Read, Write, Edit, WebSearch, WebFetch' (no Bash); content-lead.md L4 same (no Bash); taproom-ops.md L4 same (no Bash); web-engineer.md L4 'Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch' (Bash justified for build/CI work). Writer/compliance agents lack Bash; engineer has it.

claims: Four-agent roster (brand-lead, content-lead, web-engineer, taproom-ops) is defended with a concrete orphan-concern argument rather than asserted.
Routing sanity check surfaced a real ambiguity (prompt 11 — booking cancellation policy) and resolved it via description edits, not hand-waved.
Tool scoping is defensible: only web-engineer gets Bash/Grep/Glob; all four get WebSearch/WebFetch (justified for regulator verification).
COMPANY.md overtly states the sandbox path-substitution and preserves memory: project so files relocate cleanly to .claude/agents/.
Each agent cites specific UK regulators by name (Portman Group, CAP Section 18, HMRC SPR, Bristol City Council, FSA, ICO/PECR, Royal Mail prohibited-items list), not generic 'regulations'.

suggestions: Assertion 2 asks for a 'conflict resolution table' but the output uses a bulleted list covering the same ground. Consider loosening phrasing to 'section' or 'table/list' — the substantive requirement (addressing boundary edges) is met, and enforcing a table format would reward cosmetics over substance.

Assertion 11 ('Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under .claude/agent-calibration/ or agent-calibration/') is trivially satisfied when no transcripts are persisted. Consider requiring transcripts to exist OR removing the assertion — the current phrasing lets an agent pass by not producing calibration artefacts at all.

No assertion covers the quality of the routing sanity check (was it genuinely adversarial, did it catch a real edge?). This executor found a real ambiguity on prompt 11; a weaker one could pass assertion 15 with 12 trivially-routed prompts. Consider adding an assertion like 'At least one routing prompt tests a genuine boundary edge between two agents.'

No assertion covers whether the agent descriptions were revised based on the routing sanity check (closed-loop behaviour). This executor did revise two descriptions; catching that in an assertion would reward the full loop.

No assertion checks that the Identity section situates the agent in the specific domain (e.g. Bristol beer scene, Licensing Act 2003). The strong outputs do this; weak outputs would write generic bios.
eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are mostly well-calibrated and caught substantive quality. Two soft spots: the conflict-resolution-table phrasing is format-prescriptive, and the 'if persisted' clause on calibration transcripts lets absence count as compliance. All 25 assertions pass on this output, but the pass is substantive rather than lucky — the executor produced a genuinely four-agent-defended roster with a real routing-sanity flag and a resolved description tightening.
